evals.prompt: What new LLMs came out in the last week?
evals.expected_output: A grouped-by-provider digest of the most recent models from list_latest, with one-line entries (name, date, headline capability) and a 2-3 sentence narrative summary.
assertions: Output is grouped by provider, not flat
Each entry shows name, release date, and a headline capability or family note
A narrative summary at the end calls out at least one cross-cutting pattern
The digest does not invent models that are not in the list_latest response

evals.expected_output: list_latest is called with provider='anthropic', and the digest only includes Anthropic models.
assertions: Every model in the digest has provider_id='anthropic'
If only one model is returned, the response says so explicitly rather than padding with older models

evals.prompt: Give me a curated weekly brief — surface the 3 most interesting releases with full specs.
evals.expected_output: Top-line digest plus get_model calls on 3 picks (parallel) with pricing, context window, and capability flags rendered.
assertions: get_model is called exactly 3 times for the curated picks
get_model calls are issued in parallel
Curated picks are justified (not just the top 3 by date)
